```{r}
write_csv(x = data_sample_name, file = "../Output/data_sample_method2.csv")
```


####Correlation analysis 
```{r}
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, acne_severity))

```

```{r}
# Acne_NL vs Acne_L
sample_NLvsL <- metadata_final %>% 
  dplyr::filter(category_label == "Acne Lesional")
#category_label == "Acne Non-lesional" | 
data_severity_NLvsL <- data_severity %>% 
  dplyr::filter(SampleID %in% sample_NLvsL$SampleID) %>% 
  column_to_rownames("SampleID") %>% 
  select_at(vars(-one_of(nearZeroVar(., names = TRUE)))) %>% 
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

# Initialize results data frame
results <- data.frame(Metabolite = character(), Correlation = numeric(), P_value = numeric())

# Select only numeric columns (excluding 'SampleID' and 'acne_severity')
data_numeric <- data_severity_NLvsL %>%
  dplyr::select(-SampleID, -acne_severity) %>%
  dplyr::select_if(is.numeric)

# Loop over each numeric feature and calculate Spearman correlation with 'acne_severity'
for (feature in colnames(data_numeric)) {
  cor_test <- cor.test(data_numeric[[feature]], data_severity_NLvsL$acne_severity, method = "spearman")
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Metabolite = feature,
                                       Correlation = cor_test$estimate,
                                       P_value = cor_test$p.value))
}

# View results
print(results)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stats)

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing (Benjamini-Hochberg)
results$P_value_adjusted <- p.adjust(results$P_value, method = "BH")

# Filter for significant correlations (adjusted p-value < 0.05)
significant_results <- results %>% 
  filter(P_value_adjusted < 0.05) %>% 
  arrange(desc(abs(Correlation)))  # Order by absolute correlation for better visualization

# Plot significant correlations
if (nrow(significant_results) > 0) {
  ggplot(significant_results, aes(x = reorder(Metabolite, Correlation), y = Correlation)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Significant Correlations of Metabolites with Acne Severity",
         x = "Metabolite",
         y = "Spearman Correlation") +
    theme_minimal()
} else {
  cat("No significant correlations found.\n")
}

```
```{r}
library(stringr)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Join significant results with info_feature_complete
significant_results <- significant_results %>%
  left_join(info_feature_complete, by = c("Metabolite" = "Feature"))


# Ensure Compound_Name and ClassyFire#subclass are populated correctly
significant_results <- significant_results %>%
  mutate(
    Compound_Name = ifelse(is.na(Compound_Name), Metabolite, Compound_Name),  # Use Metabolite if Compound_Name is NA
    `ClassyFire#subclass` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#subclass`), "Unknown", `ClassyFire#subclass`)  # Assign "Unknown" for missing subclass
  )

# Define a custom palette with grey for "Unknown" and RdYlBu for others
palette <- c("Unknown" = "grey", scales::brewer_pal(palette = "RdYlBu")(length(unique(significant_results$`ClassyFire#subclass`)) - 1))

# Plot with Compound_Name and fallback labels
if (nrow(significant_results) > 0) {
  ggplot(significant_results, aes(x = reorder(Compound_Name, Correlation), y = Correlation, fill = `ClassyFire#subclass`)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +  # Apply custom palette
    labs(
      title = "Significant Correlations of Metabolites with Acne Severity",
      x = "Compound Name (or Metabolite ID if unavailable)",
      y = "Spearman Correlation",
      fill = "Subclass"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10))  # Adjust text size for readability
} else {
  cat("No significant correlations found.\n")
}

```
```{r}

library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Shorten Compound_Name and ensure subclass handling
significant_results <- significant_results %>%
  mutate(
    Compound_Name = ifelse(nchar(Compound_Name) > 40, paste0(substr(Compound_Name, 1, 37), "..."), Compound_Name),
    `ClassyFire#subclass` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#subclass`), "Unknown", `ClassyFire#subclass`)  # Assign "Unknown" for missing subclass
  )

library(RColorBrewer)
# Define unique subclasses
subclasses <- unique(significant_results$`ClassyFire#subclass`)

# Generate enough colors for subclasses, including "Unknown"
num_colors <- length(subclasses) - 1  # Exclude "Unknown" for color palette generation
colors <- brewer.pal(max(num_colors, 3), "RdYlBu")  # Ensure at least 3 colors for the palette
colors <- rep(colors, length.out = num_colors)  # Repeat colors if necessary to match length

# Define the palette
palette <- c("Unknown" = "grey", setNames(colors, subclasses[subclasses != "Unknown"]))

# Verify the palette
print(palette)


# Plot significant correlations
if (nrow(significant_results) > 0) {
  ggplot(significant_results, aes(x = reorder(Compound_Name, Correlation), y = Correlation, fill = `ClassyFire#subclass`)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +  # Apply custom palette
    labs(
      title = "Significant Correlations of Metabolites with Acne Severity",
      x = "Compound Name (Shortened)",
      y = "Spearman Correlation",
      fill = "Subclass"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),  # Adjust font size for readability
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 20)  # Adjust margins for better spacing
    )
} else {
  cat("No significant correlations found.\n")
}


```







```{r}
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Truncate long Compound_Name (for display purposes)
significant_results <- significant_results %>%
  mutate(
    Compound_Name = ifelse(nchar(Compound_Name) > 40, paste0(substr(Compound_Name, 1, 100), "..."), Compound_Name),
    `ClassyFire#subclass` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#subclass`), "Unknown", `ClassyFire#subclass`)  # Assign "Unknown" for missing subclass
  )

# Define unique subclasses
subclasses <- unique(significant_results$`ClassyFire#subclass`)

# Ensure enough colors for all subclasses
num_colors <- length(subclasses)  # Include "Unknown" in the count
colors <- brewer.pal(min(num_colors, 11), "RdYlBu")  # Generate up to 11 unique colors (maximum for RdYlBu)

# If more subclasses than colors, use colorRampPalette to interpolate more colors
if (num_colors > length(colors)) {
  colors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdYlBu"))(num_colors)
}

# Map colors to subclasses
palette <- setNames(colors, subclasses)

# Assign "Unknown" to grey
palette["Unknown"] <- "grey"

# Plot significant correlations
if (nrow(significant_results) > 0) {
  ggplot(significant_results, aes(y = reorder(Compound_Name, Correlation), x = Correlation, fill = `ClassyFire#subclass`)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    geom_text(
      aes(
        label = Compound_Name,
        x = ifelse(Correlation > 0, 0 - 0.05, 0 + 0.05)  # Place labels near 0
      ),
      hjust = ifelse(significant_results$Correlation > 0, 1, 0),  # Align right for positive, left for negative
      size = 3, color = "black"  # Adjust font size and color for readability
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +  # Apply custom palette
    labs(
      title = "Significant Correlations of Metabolites with Acne Severity",
      x = "Spearman Correlation",
      y = NULL,  # Remove y-axis label
      fill = "Subclass"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),  # Remove background grid
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis text
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis ticks
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 20)  # Adjust margins for better spacing
    )
} else {
  cat("No significant correlations found.\n")
}


```
```{r}
ggsave("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/spearman_cor_method1.png", plot = last_plot(), dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 6, units = "in")

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix) # for stat_compare_means
library(ggpubr)  # for ggboxplot

# Ensure `data_severity` has both the metabolite data and category label
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label))
data_severity_clr <- decostand(data_severity %>% column_to_rownames("SampleID"), method = "rclr")
# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  
  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite
  p <- data_severity %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, aes(color = category_label)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")) +
    labs(title = title, y = "Metabolite Level", x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  print(p)
}

# Optional: Perform ANOVA and Tukey HSD for each metabolite
anova_results <- list()
tukey_results <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  anova <- aov(as.formula(paste(metabolite, "~ category_label")), data = data_severity)
  anova_results[[metabolite]] <- anova
  tukey_results[[metabolite]] <- tukey_hsd(anova)
}

# You can access `anova_results` and `tukey_results` for each metabolite after running the code.

```


```{r}
# Ensure `data_severity` has both the metabolite data and category label
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label))
data_severity_clr <- decostand(data_severity %>% column_to_rownames("SampleID"), method = "rclr")
# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(feature_interest))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- feature_interest$ID[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(feature_interest$Compound_Name[i]), feature_interest$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  
  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite
  p <- data_severity %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, aes(color = category_label)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")) +
    labs(title = title, y = "Metabolite Level", x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  print(p)
}

# Optional: Perform ANOVA and Tukey HSD for each metabolite
anova_results <- list()
tukey_results <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  anova <- aov(as.formula(paste(metabolite, "~ category_label")), data = data_severity)
  anova_results[[metabolite]] <- anova
  tukey_results[[metabolite]] <- tukey_hsd(anova)
}

# You can access `anova_results` and `tukey_results` for each metabolite after running the code.
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix) # for stat_compare_means
library(ggpubr)  # for ggboxplot
library(vegan)   # for decostand

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data and category label
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID` and `category_label` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label), metabolite_data_clr)

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  
  # Define the color palette for the categories
  category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, aes(color = category_label)) +  # Use color for jitter points
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    scale_color_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set color for jitter points to match box plot
    labs(title = title, y = "Metabolite Level (CLR-transformed)", x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )

  print(p)

}

# Optional: Perform ANOVA and Tukey HSD for each metabolite on CLR-transformed data
anova_results <- list()
tukey_results <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  anova <- aov(as.formula(paste(metabolite, "~ category_label")), data = data_severity_clr)
  anova_results[[metabolite]] <- anova
  tukey_results[[metabolite]] <- tukey_hsd(anova)
}

# You can access `anova_results` and `tukey_results` for each metabolite after running the code.

```


# plot significant annotated metabolites from the PLSDA analysis
#Dec 5 return!!


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggpubr)  # For stat_compare_means()

# Join metadata with sample data
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id))

# Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- decostand(data_severity %>% column_to_rownames("SampleID") %>% select(-category_label, -host_subject_id), method = "rclr") %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID") %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id))

# Filter features with Compound_Name only
filtered_features <- feature_interest %>% filter(!is.na(Compound_Name))

# Extract mz values for each feature from info_feature_complete
info_feature_complete2 <- info_feature_complete %>% 
  dplyr::select(Feature, mz)  # Ensure you have these columns

# Custom label function for p-value and significance stars
custom_label <- function(p) {
  if (p < 0.001) {
    return(paste0("*** (", format(p, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"))
  } else if (p < 0.01) {
    return(paste0("** (", format(p, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"))
  } else if (p < 0.05) {
    return(paste0("* (", format(p, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"))
  } else {
    return(paste0("ns (", format(p, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"))
  }
}

# Define plot aesthetics
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite
for (i in seq_len(nrow(filtered_features))) {
  # Get the metabolite and compound name
  metabolite <- filtered_features$ID[i]
  metabolite_name <- filtered_features$Compound_Name[i]
  
  # Find the mz value for the current metabolite
  mz_value <- info_feature_complete2 %>% filter(Feature == metabolite) %>% pull(mz)
  
  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[as.character(metabolite)]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +  # Make box plots smaller
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +  # Make dots smaller
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", metabolite_name, "\n(mz: ", mz_value, ")", sep = ""), x = NULL) +  # Updated Y-axis label
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),  # Smaller x-axis text
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),  # Smaller y-axis text
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")  # Add x and y-axis lines
    ) +
    # Add pairwise comparison brackets with combined label
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), 
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), 
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      aes(label = custom_label(..p..)),  # Use custom combined label
      method = "wilcox.test", # instead of t.test 
      size = 3  # Smaller text for p-values and stars
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Combine and save 6 plots per figure
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  # Save as PNG in landscape format
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/metabolite_plots_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

```{r}
library(lmerTest)

# Perform repeated measures ANOVA correcting for `host_subject_id`
repeated_measures_results <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(filtered_features))) {
  metabolite <- filtered_features$ID[i]
  formula <- as.formula("value ~ category_label + (1|host_subject_id)")
  
  # Prepare the dataset for the current metabolite
  data_current <- data_severity_clr %>%
    dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id, !!sym(metabolite)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(value = !!sym(metabolite))
  
  # Run the repeated measures ANOVA
  model <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data_current)
  repeated_measures_results[[metabolite]] <- summary(model)
}

# Access the repeated measures ANOVA results
repeated_measures_results

```


```{r}
# Define the color palette for the categories
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  y_label <- paste(title, "(CLR-transformed)")  # Set dynamic y-axis label

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, aes(color = category_label)) +  # Use color for jitter points
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    scale_color_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set color for jitter points to match box plot
    labs(title = title, y = y_label, x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  print(p)
}

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define the color palette for the categories
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  y_label <- paste(title, "(CLR-transformed)")  # Set dynamic y-axis label

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional and NA for others
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    scale_color_gradient(
      low = "#f1958aff", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
      guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
    ) +
    labs(title = title, y = y_label, x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  print(p)
}



```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
dot_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  y_label <- paste(title, "(CLR-transformed)")  # Set dynamic y-axis label

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, category_label))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional and fixed colors for others
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    scale_color_manual(values = dot_colors, guide = "none") +  # Assign fixed colors for Healthy and Acne Non-lesional
    scale_color_gradient(
      low = "#f1958aff", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "#ec7064ff",  # Pink gradient for Acne Lesional severity
      guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
    ) +
    labs(title = title, y = y_label, x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Calculate rows and columns for landscape layout
num_plots <- length(all_plots)
num_cols <- 4  # Number of columns for a landscape layout
num_rows <- ceiling(num_plots / num_cols)  # Calculate required rows

# Combine all plots into one landscape figure
combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = all_plots, ncol = num_cols, nrow = num_rows, align = "v")

# Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
ggsave("combined_metabolite_boxplots.png", combined_plot, width = 14, height = num_rows * 2, dpi = 300)  # Adjust height based on rows

print(combined_plot)

```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "#f1958aff", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  y_label <- paste(title, "(CLR-transformed)")  # Set dynamic y-axis label

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(title = title, y = y_label, x = "Category") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Calculate rows and columns for landscape layout
num_plots <- length(all_plots)
num_cols <- 8  # Number of columns for a landscape layout
num_rows <- ceiling(num_plots / num_cols)  # Calculate required rows

# Combine all plots into one landscape figure
combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = all_plots, ncol = num_cols, nrow = num_rows, align = "v")

# Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
ggsave("combined_metabolite_boxplots.pdf", combined_plot, width = 20, height = num_rows * 2, dpi = 300)  # Adjust height based on rows

print(combined_plot)


```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "#f1958aff", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  title <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))
  y_label <- paste(title, "(CLR-transformed)")  # Set dynamic y-axis label

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(title = title, y = y_label, x = "") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Split `all_plots` into chunks of 20 plots each
split_plots <- split(all_plots, ceiling(seq_along(all_plots) / 10))

# Loop over each chunk and save as a separate figure
for (i in seq_along(split_plots)) {
  chunk <- split_plots[[i]]
  
  # Combine plots in the current chunk into one figure
  combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = chunk, ncol = 5, nrow = 2, align = "v", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")
  
  # Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
  file_name <- paste0("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/combined_metabolite_boxplots_part", i, ".pdf")
  ggsave(file_name, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8.5, dpi = 300)  # Adjust size for landscape layout
}

print("Plots saved successfully.")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "#f1958aff", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  y_label <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(y = paste(y_label, "(CLR-transformed)"), x = "") +  # No title, only y-axis label
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line()
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.format"  # Add p-value next to significance label
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Split `all_plots` into chunks of 10 plots each
split_plots <- split(all_plots, ceiling(seq_along(all_plots) / 10))

# Loop over each chunk and save as a separate figure
for (i in seq_along(split_plots)) {
  chunk <- split_plots[[i]]
  
  # Combine plots in the current chunk into one figure
  combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = chunk, ncol = 5, nrow = 2, align = "v", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")
  
  # Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
  file_name <- paste0("combined_metabolite_boxplots_part", i, ".pdf")
  ggsave(file_name, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8.5, dpi = 300)  # Adjust size for landscape layout
}

print("Plots saved successfully.")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "white", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  y_label <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(y = paste(y_label, "(CLR-transformed)"), x = "") +  # No title, only y-axis label
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
      plot.title = element_blank()  # Remove plot title
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      label = "p.signif",  # Add stars or ns
      label.y = max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5,  # Position stars
      size = 3
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      aes(label = paste0("p=", ..p.format..)),  # Add p-value
      label.y = max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 1,  # Position p-value above stars
      size = 2.5
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Split `all_plots` into chunks of 10 plots each
split_plots <- split(all_plots, ceiling(seq_along(all_plots) / 10))

# Loop over each chunk and save as a separate figure
for (i in seq_along(split_plots)) {
  chunk <- split_plots[[i]]
  
  # Combine plots in the current chunk into one figure
  combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = chunk, ncol = 5, nrow = 2, align = "v", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")
  
  # Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
  file_name <- paste0("combined_metabolite_boxplots_part", i, ".pdf")
  ggsave(file_name, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8.5, dpi = 300)  # Adjust size for landscape layout
}

print("Plots saved successfully.")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "white", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  y_label <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(y = paste(y_label, "(CLR-transformed)"), x = "") +  # No title, only y-axis label
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
      plot.title = element_blank(),  # Remove plot title
      panel.grid = element_blank(),  # Remove grid background
      panel.border = element_blank(),  # Remove border
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")  # Add x and y-axis lines
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      aes(label = paste(..p.signif.., "(p=", ..p.format.., ")", sep = "")),  # Combine stars and p-value
      label.y = c(
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5,
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 1,
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 1.5
      ),  # Dynamically position labels
      size = 3
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Split `all_plots` into chunks of 10 plots each
split_plots <- split(all_plots, ceiling(seq_along(all_plots) / 10))

# Loop over each chunk and save as a separate figure
for (i in seq_along(split_plots)) {
  chunk <- split_plots[[i]]
  
  # Combine plots in the current chunk into one figure
  combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = chunk, ncol = 5, nrow = 2, align = "v", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")
  
  # Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
  file_name <- paste0("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/combined_metabolite_boxplots_part", i, ".pdf")
  ggsave(file_name, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8.5, dpi = 300)  # Adjust size for landscape layout
}

print("Plots saved successfully.")


```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)

# Define color palette for dots and box plots
category_colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
severity_gradient <- scale_color_gradient(
  low = "white", high = "#c0392dff", limits = c(0, 6), na.value = "grey",
  guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Acne Severity")
)

# Prepare `data_severity` with both metabolite data, category label, and acne severity
data_severity <- data_sample %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Separate metabolite data from metadata
metabolite_data <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -acne_severity)

# Apply CLR transformation (remove metadata, apply rclr, then add metadata back)
metabolite_data_clr <- decostand(metabolite_data, method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Add back `SampleID`, `category_label`, and `acne_severity` columns
data_severity_clr <- cbind(data_severity %>% select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity), metabolite_data_clr)

# Combine all plots into one figure
all_plots <- list()

# Iterate over each metabolite in `significant_results`
for (i in seq_len(nrow(significant_results))) {
  
  # Get the metabolite and compound name (fallback to Metabolite ID if Compound_Name is missing)
  metabolite <- significant_results$Metabolite[i]
  y_label <- ifelse(!is.na(significant_results$Compound_Name[i]), significant_results$Compound_Name[i], paste("Metabolite", metabolite))

  # Create a box plot for the current metabolite using CLR-transformed data
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[metabolite]], fill = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +  # Adjust box plot width
    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8,
      aes(color = ifelse(category_label == "Acne Lesional", acne_severity, NA))
    ) +  # Use severity-based color for Acne Lesional
    scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +  # Set fill colors for box plot
    severity_gradient +  # Apply gradient scale for severity
    labs(y = paste(y_label, "(CLR-transformed)"), x = "") +  # No title, only y-axis label
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), 
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7), 
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
      plot.title = element_blank(),  # Remove plot title
      panel.grid = element_blank(),  # Remove grid background
      panel.border = element_blank(),  # Remove border
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")  # Add x and y-axis lines
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"), c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"), c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")),
      aes(label = paste(..p.signif.., "(p=", ..p.format.., ")", sep = "")),  # Combine stars and p-value
      label.y = c(
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5,
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 1,
        max(data_severity_clr[[metabolite]], na.rm = TRUE) + 1.5
      ),  # Dynamically position labels
      size = 3
    )
  
  all_plots[[i]] <- p
}

# Split `all_plots` into chunks of 10 plots each
split_plots <- split(all_plots, ceiling(seq_along(all_plots) / 10))

# Loop over each chunk and save as a separate figure
for (i in seq_along(split_plots)) {
  chunk <- split_plots[[i]]
  
  # Combine plots in the current chunk into one figure
  combined_plot <- ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = chunk, ncol = 5, nrow = 2, align = "v", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")
  
  # Save the combined plot as a landscape figure
  file_name <- paste0("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/combined_metabolite_boxplots_part", i, ".pdf")
  ggsave(file_name, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8.5, dpi = 300)  # Adjust size for landscape layout
}

print("Plots saved successfully.")

```






```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(broom.mixed)  # For extracting results from lme4 models
library(ggplot2)

data_severity <- data_sample #%>%
  #left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, category_label, acne_severity))

# Prepare the data: Ensure 'host_subject_id' and 'acne_severity' are included
data_severity_NLvsL <- data_severity %>%
  dplyr::filter(SampleID %in% sample_NLvsL$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, host_subject_id, acne_severity), by = "SampleID") %>%
  column_to_rownames("SampleID")



# Convert data types
data_severity_NLvsL$host_subject_id <- as.factor(data_severity_NLvsL$host_subject_id)
data_severity_NLvsL$acne_severity <- as.numeric(data_severity_NLvsL$acne_severity)

# Initialize results data frame for storing LMM results
results <- data.frame(Metabolite = character(), Coefficient = numeric(), P_value = numeric())

# Loop over each metabolite to fit a linear mixed-effects model
for (metabolite in colnames(data_severity_NLvsL)[!colnames(data_severity_NLvsL) %in% c("host_subject_id", "acne_severity")]) {
  # Create formula for LMM
  formula <- as.formula(paste("acne_severity ~", paste0("`", metabolite, "`"), "+ (1 | host_subject_id)"))
  
  # Fit the linear mixed-effects model
  model <- tryCatch(lmer(formula, data = data_severity_NLvsL, REML = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
  
  # Extract the fixed-effect results if the model fits successfully
  if (!is.null(model)) {
    summary_result <- broom.mixed::tidy(model)
    metabolite_result <- summary_result %>% filter(term == metabolite)
    if (nrow(metabolite_result) > 0) {
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        Metabolite = metabolite,
        Coefficient = metabolite_result$estimate,
        P_value = metabolite_result$p.value
      ))
    }
  }
}

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing using Benjamini-Hochberg method
results$P_value_adjusted <- p.adjust(results$P_value, method = "BH")

# Filter for significant results
significant_results <- results %>%
  filter(P_value_adjusted < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Coefficient)))

# Print significant results
print(significant_results)

# Visualization of significant metabolites
if (nrow(significant_results) > 0) {
  ggplot(significant_results, aes(x = reorder(Metabolite, Coefficient), y = Coefficient)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Significant Metabolites Associated with Acne Severity",
         x = "Metabolite",
         y = "LMM Coefficient") +
    theme_minimal()
} else {
  cat("No significant metabolites found.\n")
}


```







#Canopus class prediction: 

```{r}
# Extract feature IDs from column names (excluding the first column, which is SampleID)
feature_ids <- colnames(data_sample_method2)[-1]

# Filter the Canopus table to keep only rows that have feature IDs matching the feature_ids
matched_features <- canopus %>%
  filter(featureId %in% feature_ids)

# Calculate the number of features with NPC#superclass
num_features_with_superclass <- nrow(matched_features)
```


```{r}
# Calculate the range (min and max probability) of NPC#superclass probability
probability_range <- range(matched_features$`NPC#superclass Probability`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Output the results
cat("Number of features with NPC#superclass:", num_features_with_superclass, "\n")
cat("Probability range (min, max):", probability_range[1], "-", probability_range[2], "\n")
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Filter features with NPC#superclass Probability > 0.5
#adapt the probability to higher ! 0.95 and subsequently lower
matched_features <- matched_features %>%
  mutate(`NPC#superclass` = ifelse(`NPC#superclass Probability` > 0.5, `NPC#superclass`, NA)) %>%
  drop_na(`NPC#superclass`)


# Collapse peak intensities in data_sample_method2 by NPC#superclass
collapsed_table <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  # Reshape data to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SampleID, 
    names_to = "featureId", 
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  # Join with matched_features to map NPC#superclass
  left_join(matched_features, by = "featureId") %>%
  # Replace NA in NPC#superclass with "Unclassified"
  mutate(`NPC#superclass` = ifelse(is.na(`NPC#superclass`), "Unclassified", `NPC#superclass`)) %>%
  # Group by SampleID and NPC#superclass and sum intensities
  group_by(SampleID, `NPC#superclass`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Select only SampleID and category_label from metadata_final
metadata_subset <- metadata_final %>%
  select(SampleID, category_label)

# Merge category_label into collapsed_table
collapsed_table <- collapsed_table %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "SampleID")

```

```{r}
# Ensure the 'Unclassified' group is included and pie charts sum to 100%
pie_data <- collapsed_table %>%
  group_by(category_label, `NPC#superclass`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(total_intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(category_label) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate the percentage for each NPC#superclass
    percentage = total_intensity / sum(total_intensity) * 100
  ) #%>%
  # Add a row for 'Unclassified' if it doesn't exist
  #complete(`NPC#superclass` = c(unique(`NPC#superclass`), "Unclassified"), 
  #         fill = list(total_intensity = 0, percentage = 0))

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(scico) # Provides high-contrast diverging palettes

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)

# Generate a dynamic color palette using R's built-in colors
unique_superclasses <- unique(pie_data$`NPC#superclass`)

# Sort so "Unclassified" is always last
unique_superclasses <- unique_superclasses[order(unique_superclasses != "Unclassified")]

# Generate a large diverging palette using base R colors
diverging_colors <- grDevices::colors()[seq(1, length(unique_superclasses) - 1)]
# Ensure "Unclassified" is grey
palette_colors <- setNames(
  c(diverging_colors, "grey"),
  unique_superclasses
)

# Ensure "Unclassified" is last in plotting order
pie_data <- pie_data %>%
  mutate(`NPC#superclass` = fct_relevel(`NPC#superclass`, "Unclassified", after = Inf))

# Plot
a<- ggplot(pie_data, aes(x = "", y = percentage, fill = `NPC#superclass`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y") +
  facet_wrap(~category_label) +
  labs(title = "NPC#superclass Distribution by Category Label", fill = "NPC#superclass") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors)

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Define colors using ColorBrewer's "Paired" palette
max_colors <- brewer.pal.info["Paired", "maxcolors"]
color_palette <- brewer.pal(max_colors, "Paired")
color_palette <- c(color_palette, "grey")  # Add grey for "Unclassified"

# Ensure unique colors for each NPC#superclass
unique_superclasses <- unique(pie_data$`NPC#superclass`)
palette_colors <- setNames(
  rep(color_palette, length.out = length(unique_superclasses)),
  unique_superclasses
)

# 1. Plot Including "Unclassified"
plot_with_unclassified <- ggplot(pie_data, aes(x = category_label, y = percentage, fill = `NPC#superclass`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "NPC#superclass Proportions with NPC#superclass Probability > 0.5 Including Unclassified",
    y = "Proportion",
    x = "Category Label",
    fill = "NPC#superclass"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 2. Plot Excluding "Unclassified"
pie_data_excluding_unclassified <- pie_data %>%
  filter(`NPC#superclass` != "Unclassified")

plot_excluding_unclassified <- ggplot(pie_data_excluding_unclassified, aes(x = category_label, y = percentage, fill = `NPC#superclass`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "NPC#superclass Proportions with NPC#superclass Probability > 0.5 Excluding Unclassified",
    y = "Proportion",
    x = "Category Label",
    fill = "NPC#superclass"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3. Plot Focusing on Top 10 Categories
top_categories <- pie_data_excluding_unclassified %>%
  group_by(`NPC#superclass`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(total_intensity), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_intensity)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(`NPC#superclass`)

pie_data_top_10 <- pie_data_excluding_unclassified %>%
  filter(`NPC#superclass` %in% top_categories)

plot_top_10 <- ggplot(pie_data_top_10, aes(x = category_label, y = percentage, fill = `NPC#superclass`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportions of Top 10 NPC#superclass Categories with NPC#superclass Probability > 0.5  ",
    y = "Proportion",
    x = "Category Label",
    fill = "NPC#superclass"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plots
print(plot_with_unclassified)
print(plot_excluding_unclassified)
print(plot_top_10)

```

```{r}
ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/plot_excluding_unclassified", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = plot_excluding_unclassified,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Prepare data for rclr transformation
data_severity <- collapsed_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `NPC#superclass`, values_from = total_intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 2: Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- data_severity%>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(SampleID = data_severity$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Generate box plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each NPC#superclass
for (feature in colnames(data_severity_clr)[!colnames(data_severity_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", feature), x = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 4: Save box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/rclr_boxplots_NPC_superclass_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```




```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Filter for Eicosanoids
eicosanoid_features <- matched_features %>%
  filter(`NPC#superclass` == "Eicosanoids") %>%
  pull(featureId)

# Step 2: Prepare Data for rclr Transformation
data_eicosanoids <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  select(SampleID, all_of(eicosanoid_features)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -SampleID, names_to = "featureId", values_to = "intensity") %>%
  group_by(SampleID) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = featureId, values_from = intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Perform rclr Transformation
data_eicosanoids_clr <- data_eicosanoids %>%
  column_to_rownames("SampleID") %>%  # Move SampleID to row names
  select(-category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID") %>%  # Restore SampleID from row names
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")


# Step 4: Generate Box Plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over Eicosanoid Features
for (feature in colnames(data_eicosanoids_clr)[!colnames(data_eicosanoids_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_eicosanoids_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\nFeature:", feature), x = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",   # Use Wilcoxon test
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 5: Save Box Plots in Grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 2
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 2)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/rclr_boxplots_eicosanoids_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

```{r}
# Iterate over Eicosanoid Features (non-CLR-transformed)
plots <- list()
for (feature in colnames(data_eicosanoids)[!colnames(data_eicosanoids) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_eicosanoids %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste(feature), x = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",  # Use Wilcoxon test
      p.adjust.method = "BH",  # Apply Benjamini-Hochberg correction
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Save the box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 2
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 2)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/non_clr_boxplots_eicosanoids_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

```{r}
# Check data types
str(data_eicosanoids_clr)

# Ensure category_label is a factor
data_eicosanoids_clr$category_label <- as.factor(data_eicosanoids_clr$category_label)


```
```{r}
# Example for feature "24058"
feature_to_check <- "24058"

wilcox_result <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
  x = data_eicosanoids_clr[[feature_to_check]], 
  g = data_eicosanoids_clr$category_label, 
  p.adjust.method = "none"
)

print(wilcox_result)

```
```{r}
# Iterate over all features
results <- list()
for (feature in colnames(data_eicosanoids_clr)[!colnames(data_eicosanoids_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Ensure no NA values
  test_data <- data_eicosanoids_clr %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[feature]]))
  
  # Perform Wilcoxon test
  wilcox_result <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    x = test_data[[feature]],
    g = test_data$category_label,
    p.adjust.method = "none"
  )
  
  # Store results
  results[[feature]] <- wilcox_result
}

# Print the results for each feature
for (feature in names(results)) {
  cat("\nFeature:", feature, "\n")
  print(results[[feature]])
}

```

#USE CLASSYFIRE class

```{r}
# Extract feature IDs from column names (excluding the first column, which is SampleID)
feature_ids <- colnames(data_sample_method2)[-1]

# Filter the Canopus table to keep only rows that have feature IDs matching the feature_ids
matched_features <- canopus %>%
  filter(featureId %in% feature_ids)

# Calculate the number of features with NPC#superclass
num_features_with_subclass <- nrow(matched_features)
```

```{r}
# Calculate the range (min and max probability) of ClassyFire#subclass probability
probability_range <- range(matched_features$`ClassyFire#subclass Probability`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Output the results
cat("Number of features with ClassyFire#subclass:", num_features_with_subclass, "\n")
cat("Probability range (min, max):", probability_range[1], "-", probability_range[2], "\n")

```

```{r}
# Step 1: Filter features with ClassyFire#subclass Probability > 0.95
# Adapt the probability to higher (>0.95) and subsequently lower thresholds
matched_features <- matched_features %>%
  mutate(`ClassyFire#subclass` = ifelse(`ClassyFire#subclass Probability` > 0.80, `ClassyFire#subclass`, NA)) %>%
  drop_na(`ClassyFire#subclass`)

# Collapse peak intensities in data_sample_method2 by ClassyFire#subclass
collapsed_table <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  # Reshape data to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SampleID, 
    names_to = "featureId", 
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  # Join with matched_features to map ClassyFire#subclass
  left_join(matched_features, by = "featureId") %>%
  # Replace NA in ClassyFire#subclass with "Unclassified"
  mutate(`ClassyFire#subclass` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#subclass`), "Unclassified", `ClassyFire#subclass`)) %>%
  # Group by SampleID and ClassyFire#subclass and sum intensities
  group_by(SampleID, `ClassyFire#subclass`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Select only SampleID and category_label from metadata_final
metadata_subset <- metadata_final %>%
  select(SampleID, category_label)

# Merge category_label into collapsed_table
collapsed_table <- collapsed_table %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "SampleID")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Prepare data for rclr transformation
data_severity <- collapsed_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `ClassyFire#subclass`, values_from = total_intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 2: Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(SampleID = data_severity$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Generate box plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each ClassyFire#subclass
for (feature in colnames(data_severity_clr)[!colnames(data_severity_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", feature), x = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 4: Save box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/ClassyFire/subclass_80/rclr_boxplots_ClassyFire_subclass_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```
## Adapt for class

```{r}
# Extract feature IDs from column names (excluding the first column, which is SampleID)
feature_ids <- colnames(data_sample_method2)[-1]

# Filter the Canopus table to keep only rows that have feature IDs matching the feature_ids
matched_features <- canopus %>%
  filter(featureId %in% feature_ids)

# Calculate the number of features with ClassyFire#class
num_features_with_class <- nrow(matched_features)

```

```{r}
# Calculate the range (min and max probability) of ClassyFire#class probability
probability_range <- range(matched_features$`ClassyFire#class Probability`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Output the results
cat("Number of features with ClassyFire#class:", num_features_with_class, "\n")
cat("Probability range (min, max):", probability_range[1], "-", probability_range[2], "\n")

```
```{r}
# Step 1: Filter features with ClassyFire#class Probability > 0.80
matched_features <- matched_features %>%
  mutate(`ClassyFire#class` = ifelse(`ClassyFire#class Probability` > 0.75, `ClassyFire#class`, NA)) %>%
  drop_na(`ClassyFire#class`)

# Collapse peak intensities in data_sample_method2 by ClassyFire#class
collapsed_table <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  # Reshape data to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SampleID, 
    names_to = "featureId", 
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  # Join with matched_features to map ClassyFire#class
  left_join(matched_features, by = "featureId") %>%
  # Replace NA in ClassyFire#class with "Unclassified"
  mutate(`ClassyFire#class` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#class`), "Unclassified", `ClassyFire#class`)) %>%
  # Group by SampleID and ClassyFire#class and sum intensities
  group_by(SampleID, `ClassyFire#class`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Select only SampleID and category_label from metadata_final
metadata_subset <- metadata_final %>%
  select(SampleID, category_label)

# Merge category_label into collapsed_table
collapsed_table <- collapsed_table %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "SampleID")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Prepare data for rclr transformation
data_severity <- collapsed_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `ClassyFire#class`, values_from = total_intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 2: Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(SampleID = data_severity$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Generate box plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each ClassyFire#class
for (feature in colnames(data_severity_clr)[!colnames(data_severity_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", feature), x = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 4: Save box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/ClassyFire/class_75/rclr_boxplots_ClassyFire_class_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

## ClassyFire superclass
```{r}
# Extract feature IDs from column names (excluding the first column, which is SampleID)
feature_ids <- colnames(data_sample_method2)[-1]

# Filter the Canopus table to keep only rows that have feature IDs matching the feature_ids
matched_features <- canopus %>%
  filter(featureId %in% feature_ids)

# Calculate the number of features with ClassyFire#superclass
num_features_with_superclass <- nrow(matched_features)

```

```{r}
# Calculate the range (min and max probability) of ClassyFire#superclass probability
probability_range <- range(matched_features$`ClassyFire#superclass probability`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Output the results
cat("Number of features with ClassyFire#superclass:", num_features_with_superclass, "\n")
cat("Probability range (min, max):", probability_range[1], "-", probability_range[2], "\n")

```

```{r}
# Step 1: Filter features with ClassyFire#superclass Probability > 0.80
matched_features <- matched_features %>%
  mutate(`ClassyFire#superclass` = ifelse(`ClassyFire#superclass probability` > 0.75, `ClassyFire#superclass`, NA)) %>%
  drop_na(`ClassyFire#superclass`)

# Collapse peak intensities in data_sample_method2 by ClassyFire#superclass
collapsed_table <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  # Reshape data to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SampleID, 
    names_to = "featureId", 
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  # Join with matched_features to map ClassyFire#superclass
  left_join(matched_features, by = "featureId") %>%
  # Replace NA in ClassyFire#superclass with "Unclassified"
  mutate(`ClassyFire#superclass` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#superclass`), "Unclassified", `ClassyFire#superclass`)) %>%
  # Group by SampleID and ClassyFire#superclass and sum intensities
  group_by(SampleID, `ClassyFire#superclass`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Select only SampleID and category_label from metadata_final
metadata_subset <- metadata_final %>%
  select(SampleID, category_label)

# Merge category_label into collapsed_table
collapsed_table <- collapsed_table %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "SampleID")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Prepare data for rclr transformation
data_severity <- collapsed_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `ClassyFire#superclass`, values_from = total_intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 2: Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(SampleID = data_severity$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Generate box plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each ClassyFire#superclass
```
