```{r}
for (feature in colnames(data_severity_clr)[!colnames(data_severity_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", feature), x = NULL, title = feature) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      title = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = counts$label_with_count)+
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 4: Save box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/ClassyFire/superclass_75/rclr_boxplots_ClassyFire_superclass_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

### ClassyFire most specific class

```{r}
# Extract feature IDs from column names (excluding the first column, which is SampleID)
feature_ids <- colnames(data_sample_method2)[-1]

# Filter the Canopus table to keep only rows that have feature IDs matching the feature_ids
matched_features <- canopus %>%
  filter(featureId %in% feature_ids)

# Calculate the number of features with ClassyFire#most specific class
num_features_with_class <- nrow(matched_features)

```

```{r}
# Calculate the range (min and max probability) of ClassyFire#most specific class probability
probability_range <- range(matched_features$`ClassyFire#most specific class Probability`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Output the results
cat("Number of features with ClassyFire#most specific class:", num_features_with_class, "\n")
cat("Probability range (min, max):", probability_range[1], "-", probability_range[2], "\n")

```
```{r}
# Step 1: Filter features with ClassyFire#most specific class Probability > 0.75
matched_features <- matched_features %>%
  mutate(`ClassyFire#most specific class` = ifelse(`ClassyFire#most specific class Probability` > 0.75, `ClassyFire#most specific class`, NA)) %>%
  drop_na(`ClassyFire#most specific class`)

# Collapse peak intensities in data_sample_method2 by ClassyFire#most specific class
collapsed_table <- data_sample_method2 %>%
  # Reshape data to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SampleID, 
    names_to = "featureId", 
    values_to = "intensity"
  ) %>%
  # Join with matched_features to map ClassyFire#most specific class
  left_join(matched_features, by = "featureId") %>%
  # Replace NA in ClassyFire#most specific class with "Unclassified"
  mutate(`ClassyFire#most specific class` = ifelse(is.na(`ClassyFire#most specific class`), "Unclassified", `ClassyFire#most specific class`)) %>%
  # Group by SampleID and ClassyFire#most specific class and sum intensities
  group_by(SampleID, `ClassyFire#most specific class`) %>%
  summarise(total_intensity = sum(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Select only SampleID and category_label from metadata_final
metadata_subset <- metadata_final %>%
  select(SampleID, category_label)

# Merge category_label into collapsed_table
collapsed_table <- collapsed_table %>%
  left_join(metadata_subset, by = "SampleID")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(vegan)  # For rclr transformation

# Step 1: Prepare data for rclr transformation
data_severity <- collapsed_table %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `ClassyFire#most specific class`, values_from = total_intensity, values_fill = 0) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 2: Perform rclr transformation
data_severity_clr <- data_severity %>%
  select(-SampleID, -category_label, -host_subject_id) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  decostand(method = "rclr") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(SampleID = data_severity$SampleID) %>%
  left_join(metadata_final %>% select(SampleID, category_label, host_subject_id), by = "SampleID")

# Step 3: Generate box plots
colors <- c("Healthy" = "#423fa6", "Acne Non-lesional" = "#67b2be", "Acne Lesional" = "#dd7963")
plots <- list()

# Iterate over each ClassyFire#most specific class
for (feature in colnames(data_severity_clr)[!colnames(data_severity_clr) %in% c("SampleID", "category_label", "host_subject_id")]) {
  # Create a box plot for the current feature
  p <- data_severity_clr %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = category_label, y = .data[[feature]], fill = category_label, color = category_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", width = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
    labs(y = paste("rclr Transformed\n", feature), x = NULL, title = feature) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
      title = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.ticks = element_line(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
    ) + scale_x_discrete(labels = counts$label_with_count) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(
        c("Acne Lesional", "Healthy"),
        c("Acne Lesional", "Acne Non-lesional"),
        c("Healthy", "Acne Non-lesional")
      ),
      method = "wilcox.test",
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      size = 3
    )
  
  plots[[length(plots) + 1]] <- p
}

# Step 4: Save box plots in grids of 6
n_per_figure <- 6
plot_index <- 1

for (i in seq(1, length(plots), by = n_per_figure)) {
  figure <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots[i:min(i + n_per_figure - 1, length(plots))], ncol = 3)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../Figures/rclr_Figures/ClassyFire/most_specific_class_90/rclr_boxplots_ClassyFire_most_specific_class_page_", plot_index, ".png"),
    plot = figure,
    width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300
  )
  plot_index <- plot_index + 1
}

```

#### MATCHES with MASST 

```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(jsonlite)

# Load the JSON file
file_path <- "/Users/bpessemier/software/masst_env2/microbe_masst/L_Oreal/output_run3_10252024/gnps2_run3_11212024_32888_combined.json" # Replace with your file path
#file_path <- "/Users/bpessemier/software/masst_env2/microbe_masst/L_Oreal/output_run3_10252024/gnps2_run3_11212024_32825_combined.json"
data <- fromJSON(file_path)

# Extract the children dataframe where the relevant categories are stored
category_data <- data$children

# Create a summary of matches by category
if (!is.null(category_data)) {
  category_matches <- data.frame(
    Category = category_data$name,
    Matched_Size = category_data$matched_size
  )
  
  # Print the results
  print(category_matches)
} else {
  cat("No category data found.\n")
}
```

```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(jsonlite)

# Load the JSON file
file_path <- "/Users/bpessemier/software/masst_env2/microbe_masst/L_Oreal/output_run3_10252024/gnps2_run3_11212024_32888_combined.json"
data <- fromJSON(file_path)

# Extract matches for the main categories and drill down into "global" for "skin"
extract_matches_with_skin <- function(data) {
  results <- data.frame(Category = character(), Matched_Size = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # Extract main categories
  if (!is.null(data$children)) {
    for (i in 1:nrow(data$children)) {
      category <- data$children$name[i]
      matched_size <- data$children$matched_size[i]
      
      # Add to results
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category = category, Matched_Size = matched_size))
      
      # Check for "global" category to drill down for "skin"
      if (category == "global" && !is.null(data$children$children[[i]])) {
        for (j in 1:nrow(data$children$children[[i]])) {
          sub_category <- data$children$children[[i]]$name[j]
          sub_matched_size <- data$children$children[[i]]$matched_size[j]
          
          # Look for "skin"
          if (sub_category == "skin") {
            results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category = sub_category, Matched_Size = sub_matched_size))
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  return(results)
}

# Run the extraction function
matches_with_skin <- extract_matches_with_skin(data)

# Print the results
print(matches_with_skin)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)

# Define the directory containing the JSON files
directory_path <- "/Users/bpessemier/software/masst_env2/microbe_masst/L_Oreal/output_run3_10252024"

# List all _combined.json files in the directory
json_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "_combined\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty data frame to store the results
all_matches <- data.frame(
  Compound_ID = character(),
  Category = character(),
  Matched_Size = integer(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to extract matches from a JSON file
extract_matches <- function(file_path) {
  # Read the JSON file
  data <- fromJSON(file_path)
  
  # Extract the compound ID from the file name
  compound_id <- sub(".*_(\\d+)_combined\\.json$", "\\1", basename(file_path))
  
  # Initialize a data frame to store the results for this compound
  matches <- data.frame(
    Compound_ID = character(),
    Category = character(),
    Matched_Size = integer(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Check if 'children' exists
  if (!is.null(data$children)) {
    # Iterate over top-level categories
    for (i in seq_along(data$children$name)) {
      category <- data$children$name[i]
      matched_size <- data$children$matched_size[i]
      
      # Append the result
      matches <- rbind(matches, data.frame(
        Compound_ID = compound_id,
        Category = category,
        Matched_Size = matched_size,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
      
      # Drill down into 'global' category to find 'skin'
      if (category == "global" && !is.null(data$children$children[[i]])) {
        for (j in seq_along(data$children$children[[i]]$name)) {
          sub_category <- data$children$children[[i]]$name[j]
          sub_matched_size <- data$children$children[[i]]$matched_size[j]
          
          if (tolower(sub_category) == "skin") {
            matches <- rbind(matches, data.frame(
              Compound_ID = compound_id,
              Category = "global > skin",
              Matched_Size = sub_matched_size,
              stringsAsFactors = FALSE
            ))
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(matches)
}

# Loop through each JSON file and extract matches
for (file in json_files) {
  file_matches <- extract_matches(file)
  all_matches <- bind_rows(all_matches, file_matches)
}

# Print the combined results
print(all_matches)

```




```{r}
# Create a list of unique categories
unique_categories <- unique(all_matches$Category)

# Initialize the binary matrix with zeros
binary_matrix <- data.frame(
  Compound_ID = unique(all_matches$Compound_ID),
  matrix(0, nrow = length(unique(all_matches$Compound_ID)), ncol = length(unique_categories)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

```
```{r}
# Assign column names to the binary matrix
colnames(binary_matrix)[-1] <- unique_categories

# Fill the binary matrix
for (i in 1:nrow(all_matches)) {
  compound <- all_matches$Compound_ID[i]
  category <- all_matches$Category[i]
  binary_matrix[binary_matrix$Compound_ID == compound, category] <- 1
}
```
```{r}
# Remove the 'Compound_ID' column for the UpSet plot
binary_matrix_for_upset <- binary_matrix[,-1]
# Assign custom colors to each set
#custom_colors <- c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "red","steelblue", "darkred", "black")  # Example colors


# Generate the UpSet plot
upset(
  binary_matrix_for_upset,
  sets = colnames(binary_matrix_for_upset),
  order.by = "freq",
  main.bar.color = "steelblue",
  sets.bar.color = "darkred",
  matrix.color = "black",
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5)
)

```
```{r}
# Define your sets
sets <- c("food", "microbes", "personal care product", "global > skin", "microbiome", "global", "plants")

# Assign custom colors to each set
custom_colors <- c("#393a92", "#76325f", "#e8bad8", "#cdc787", "#f08976","#3391c1","#458550")  

# Create the metadata data frame
set_metadata <- data.frame(
  sets = sets,
  colors = custom_colors,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(UpSetR)

# Assuming 'binary_matrix' is your data frame prepared for the UpSet plot

# Generate the UpSet plot with custom set size bar colors
UpSetR::upset(
  binary_matrix,
  sets = sets,
  keep.order = TRUE,
  set.metadata = list(
    data = set_metadata,
    plots = list(
      list(
        type = "matrix_rows",
        column = "colors",
        colors = setNames(custom_colors, sets)
      )
    )
  ),
  order.by = c("freq", "degree")
)

```

```{r}
  sets = sets,
  colors = custom_colors,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

  binary_matrix,

  set.metadata = list(
    data = set_metadata,
    plots = list(
      list(
        type = "matrix_rows",

)
```

```{r}
# Extract the 'sets' vector from set_metadata
metadata_sets <- set_metadata$sets

# Extract the column names from binary_matrix (excluding the 'FeatureID' column if present)
matrix_sets <- colnames(binary_matrix)[colnames(binary_matrix) != "Compound_ID"]

# Check if the orders are identical
identical(metadata_sets, matrix_sets)

```

```{r}
# Create a factor with levels in the order of matrix_sets
set_metadata$sets <- factor(set_metadata$sets, levels = matrix_sets)

# Reorder set_metadata based on the factor levels
set_metadata <- set_metadata[order(set_metadata$sets), ]

# Convert 'sets' back to character if necessary
set_metadata$sets <- as.character(set_metadata$sets)

```

```{r}
library(UpSetR)

upset(
  binary_matrix,
  sets = matrix_sets,
  keep.order = TRUE,
  set.metadata = list(
    data = set_metadata,
    plots = list(
      list(
        type = "matrix_rows",
        column = "colors",
        colors = setNames(set_metadata$colors, set_metadata$sets)
      )
    )
  ),
  order.by = c("freq", "degree")
)
```

```{r}
# Generate the UpSet plot with custom set size bar colors
ComplexUpset::upset(
  binary_matrix,
  intersect = sets,
  base_annotations = list(
    'Set size' = (
      ggplot(mapping = aes(x = intersection, fill = intersection)) +
      geom_bar() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors)
    )
  )
)
```

```{r}
      geom = geom_bar(

        width = 0.6
```

```{r}
    upset_query(set = "global", fill = custom_colors["global"]),
    upset_query(set = "global > skin", fill = custom_colors["global > skin"]),
    upset_query(set = "food", fill = custom_colors["food"]),
    upset_query(set = "microbes", fill = custom_colors["microbes"]),
    upset_query(set = "microbiome", fill = custom_colors["microbiome"]),
    upset_query(set = "personal care product", fill = custom_colors["personal care product"])
  )
)
```


```{r}
# Drop the unwanted column 'global > skin' from the binary matrix
binary_matrix_backup <- binary_matrix

binary_matrix<- binary_matrix[, !colnames(binary_matrix) %in% "global > skin"]

```

```{r}
# Define the sets of interest
#sets_of_interest <- c("personal care product", "global > skin", "global")
#sets_of_interest <- c("microbes", "food")
#sets_of_interest <- c("global", "personal care product")

#sets_of_interest <- c("microbes", "personal care product")
#sets_of_interest <- c("global", "plants", "food")
sets_of_interest <- c("Humans", "Human skin", "global","personal care product")
# Alternatively: sets_of_interest <- c("microbes", "food")

# Ensure binary_matrix is a data frame
binary_matrix <- as.data.frame(binary_matrix)

# Identify the columns corresponding to the sets of interest
interest_columns <- binary_matrix[, sets_of_interest, drop = FALSE]

# Ensure interest columns are numeric
interest_columns <- sapply(interest_columns, as.numeric)

# Sum the presence across the sets of interest
interest_presence_sum <- rowSums(interest_columns)

# Identify columns corresponding to other sets (exclude `Compound_ID`)
other_sets <- setdiff(colnames(binary_matrix), c("Compound_ID", sets_of_interest))
other_set_columns <- binary_matrix[, other_sets, drop = FALSE]

# Ensure other set columns are numeric
other_set_columns <- sapply(other_set_columns, as.numeric)

# Calculate row sums for the other sets
other_set_presence_sum <- rowSums(other_set_columns)

# Filter for metabolites present in all specified sets and absent in others
exclusive_metabolites <- binary_matrix[
  interest_presence_sum == length(sets_of_interest) & other_set_presence_sum == 0,
  "Compound_ID"
]

# Display the result
print(exclusive_metabolites)

```

```{r}
  binary_matrix,

    upset_query(set = "plants", fill = custom_colors["plants"]),
    upset_query(set = "global", fill = custom_colors["global"]),
    upset_query(set = "food", fill = custom_colors["food"]),

)
```

#RERUN and correct for the extraction of Humans and human skin metabolites
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the directory containing the files
directory_path <- "/Users/bpessemier/software/masst_env2/microbe_masst/L_Oreal/output_run3_10252024"

# List all _combined.json and _global.tsv files in the directory
json_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "_combined\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)
tsv_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "_counts_global\\.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty data frame to store the results
all_matches <- data.frame(
  Compound_ID = character(),
  Category = character(),
  Matched_Size = integer(),
  Homo_sapiens = logical(),
  Human_skin = logical(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to extract matches from a JSON file
extract_matches <- function(file_path) {
  # Read the JSON file
  data <- fromJSON(file_path)
  
  # Extract the compound ID from the file name
  compound_id <- sub(".*_(\\d+)_combined\\.json$", "\\1", basename(file_path))
  
  # Initialize a data frame to store the results for this compound
  matches <- data.frame(
    Compound_ID = character(),
    Category = character(),
    Matched_Size = integer(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Check if 'children' exists
  if (!is.null(data$children)) {
    # Iterate over top-level categories
    for (i in seq_along(data$children$name)) {
      category <- data$children$name[i]
      matched_size <- data$children$matched_size[i]
      
      # Append the result
      matches <- rbind(matches, data.frame(
        Compound_ID = compound_id,
        Category = category,
        Matched_Size = matched_size,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  return(matches)
}

# Function to extract information from a global.tsv file
extract_tsv_info <- function(file_path) {
  # Read the TSV file
  data <- read_tsv(file_path, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))
  
  # Check for `9606|Homo sapiens` and `Human skin`
  homo_sapiens_present <- any(grepl("9606\\|Homo sapiens", data$NCBITaxonomy, ignore.case = TRUE))
  human_skin_present <- any(
    grepl("9606\\|Homo sapiens", data$NCBITaxonomy, ignore.case = TRUE) &
    grepl("skin", data$UBERONBodyPartName, ignore.case = TRUE)
  )
  
  # Extract the compound ID from the file name
  compound_id <- sub(".*_(\\d+)_counts_global\\.tsv$", "\\1", basename(file_path))
  
  # Return a data frame with the results
  return(data.frame(
    Compound_ID = compound_id,
    Homo_sapiens = homo_sapiens_present,
    Human_skin = human_skin_present,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}


# Loop through JSON files and extract matches
for (file in json_files) {
  file_matches <- extract_matches(file)
  all_matches <- bind_rows(all_matches, file_matches)
}

# Loop through TSV files and extract additional information
tsv_info <- lapply(tsv_files, extract_tsv_info) %>% bind_rows()

# Merge JSON and TSV results
final_results <- left_join(all_matches, tsv_info, by = "Compound_ID")

# Print the combined results
print(final_results)

# Save the results to a CSV file
write.csv(final_results, file = "masst_screening_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

#first removed Homo sapiens and Humans column before running this
```{r}
# Define the directory containing the TSV files
tsv_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "_counts_global\\.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Create a lookup table for Homo sapiens and Human skin from global.tsv files
homo_sapiens_skin_info <- data.frame(
  Compound_ID = character(),
  Homo_sapiens = logical(),
  Human_skin = logical(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to process each global.tsv file
extract_global_info <- function(file_path) {
  # Read the TSV file
  data <- read_tsv(file_path, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))
  
  # Extract the compound ID from the file name
  compound_id <- sub(".*_(\\d+)_counts_global\\.tsv$", "\\1", basename(file_path))
  
  # Check for Homo sapiens and Human skin
  homo_sapiens_present <- any(grepl("9606\\|Homo sapiens", data$NCBITaxonomy, ignore.case = TRUE))
  human_skin_present <- any(
    grepl("9606\\|Homo sapiens", data$NCBITaxonomy, ignore.case = TRUE) &
    grepl("skin", data$UBERONBodyPartName, ignore.case = TRUE)
  )
  
  # Return a single-row data frame with the results
  return(data.frame(
    Compound_ID = compound_id,
    Homo_sapiens = homo_sapiens_present,
    Human_skin = human_skin_present,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

# Loop through all global.tsv files and collect information
for (file in tsv_files) {
  tsv_info <- extract_global_info(file)
  homo_sapiens_skin_info <- bind_rows(homo_sapiens_skin_info, tsv_info)
}

# Update `all_matches` with values for Homo_sapiens and Human_skin


# Merge with `homo_sapiens_skin_info` and update values
all_matches <- all_matches %>%
  left_join(homo_sapiens_skin_info, by = "Compound_ID") %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure Homo_sapiens and Human_skin are only relevant for the 'global' category
    Homo_sapiens = ifelse(Category == "global", Homo_sapiens, NA),
    Human_skin = ifelse(Category == "global", Human_skin, NA)
  )

# Print the final results
print(all_matches)

# Save the results to a CSV file
write.csv(all_matches, file = "masst_screening_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)




```


```{r}
# Step 1: Add Subcategories
all_matches <- all_matches %>%
  bind_rows(
    all_matches %>%
      filter(Homo_sapiens == TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Category = "Humans", Matched_Size = 1),
    all_matches %>%
      filter(Human_skin == TRUE) %>%
      mutate(Category = "Human skin", Matched_Size = 1)
  )

# Step 2: Create the Binary Matrix
# Create a list of unique categories
unique_categories <- unique(all_matches$Category)

# Initialize the binary matrix
binary_matrix <- data.frame(
  Compound_ID = unique(all_matches$Compound_ID),
  matrix(0, nrow = length(unique(all_matches$Compound_ID)), ncol = length(unique_categories)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Rename columns to include the categories
colnames(binary_matrix)[-1] <- unique_categories

# Fill in the binary matrix
for (i in seq_len(nrow(binary_matrix))) {
  compound_id <- binary_matrix$Compound_ID[i]
  matched_categories <- all_matches$Category[all_matches$Compound_ID == compound_id]
  binary_matrix[i, matched_categories] <- 1
}

# View the binary matrix
print(binary_matrix)

```
```{r}
# Define your sets
sets <- c("Plants",  "Humans", "Human skin",  "Food", "Microbes", "Microbiome", "Personal Care Product")

# Assign custom colors to each set
custom_colors <- c(
  "Humans" = "#cdc787", 
  "Human skin" ="#393a92",#"#efa370",
  "Plants" = "#458550",
  #"global" = "#393a92",
  "Food" = "#3391c1",
  "Microbes" = "#76325f",
  "Microbiome" = "#f08976",
  "Personal Care Product" = "#e8bad8"
)
```

```{r}
# Rename the sets
colnames(binary_matrix) <- c("Compound_ID", "Plants", "Food", "Microbes", "Microbiome", "Personal Care Product", "Humans", "Human skin")


```


```{r}
# Generate the UpSet plot with custom colors for specific sets
upset_plot <- ComplexUpset::upset(
  binary_matrix,
  intersect = sets,
  queries = list(
    upset_query(set = "Plants", fill = custom_colors["Plants"]),
    #upset_query(set = "global", fill = custom_colors["global"]),
    upset_query(set = "Food", fill = custom_colors["Food"]),
    upset_query(set = "Microbes", fill = custom_colors["Microbes"]),
    upset_query(set = "Microbiome", fill = custom_colors["Microbiome"]),
    upset_query(set = "Personal Care Product", fill = custom_colors["Personal Care Product"]), 
    upset_query(set = "Humans", fill = custom_colors["Humans"]),
    upset_query(set = "Human skin", fill = custom_colors["Human skin"])
  ),
  sort_intersections = c("descending") # Order intersections by degree and then frequency
)
```


```{r}
ggsave("../Figures/metabolomics_Figures/upset_masst_human_humanskin.png", plot = last_plot(), dpi = 300, width = 14, height = 6, units = "in")
```
